name: Placental growth-rate parameter
tier_rationale: Tier C from Carter 2009 Placenta: review of factors affecting placental O2 transfer including the rate of villous surface-area expansion. Logistic-growth rate parameter is a model fit.
notes: Logistic growth-rate coefficient for the villous surface-area trajectory. Caveats: Logistic-shape fit parameter; not reported directly in source.

name: Initial villous surface area
tier_rationale: Tier B from Mayhew & Hannan 2015 Placenta 'Morphomics' review: human-placenta villous surface area at early gestation from stereological studies. Replicated across multiple morphometric papers.

applicability.population: Stereological measurements of human placentae across gestation

notes: Effective villous exchange surface area at the start of gestation (early-pregnancy reference).

name: Villous membrane thickness at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Mayhew & Hannan 2015 Placenta 'Morphomics' review: harmonic-mean villous-membrane thickness at term, the most important determinant of placental O2 diffusing capacity (Mayhew 1986 morphometric analysis).
notes: Arithmetic mean villous membrane thickness at term.

name: Placental growth midpoint week
tier_rationale: Tier C from Carter 2009 Placenta: gestational week at which villous surface area reaches the logistic-curve midpoint. Model-fit parameter.
notes: Gestational age at which villous-area logistic growth reaches its inflection point. Caveats: Logistic-shape fit parameter; not reported directly in source.

name: Term villous surface area
tier_rationale: Tier B from Mayhew & Hannan 2015 'Morphomics' (PMID 25637410): stereological synthesis of villous surface-area data; term value ~11-13 m^2. Burton 2010 (PMID 26428504) provides the broader placental-anatomy context for villous-tree expansion.
notes: Effective villous exchange surface area at term.

name: Placental weight at term
tier_rationale: Tier B: term human placental weight ~470 g is the canonical clinical-pathology figure, widely reported in obstetric and perinatal pathology references. Mayhew & Hannan 2015 'Morphomics' (PMID 25637410) and Burton 2010 (PMID 26428504) describe the placenta's structural metrics including mass. Wide cohort variability (380-560 g 1σ range) reflects real biological variation.

extraction.method: triangulated across pathology and morphometric references

name: Placenta-to-fetus weight ratio at term
tier_rationale: Tier B: placental:fetal weight ratio at term ~0.14 (i.e., ~1:7) is well-characterised across obstetric pathology cohorts. Computed as ~470 g placenta / ~3350 g term fetus. Ratio is elevated in IUGR (placenta proportionally larger relative to fetus) and reduced in macrosomia. Burton 2010 (PMID 26428504) discusses placental-fetal mass relationships.

extraction.method: derived from term placental weight and term fetal weight; needs PDF cross-check

notes: Ratio shifts in growth-discordant pregnancies; used clinically as a placental-function index.

name: Placental ~ fetal weight allometric prefactor (a in PW = a * FW^b)
tier_rationale: Tier C: derived from Hutcheon 2012 (PMID 22845665) pooled placental/fetal weight regression and Burton 2010 placental-development review. PW(3500g) = 0.4 * 3500^0.85 ~ 494 g, matching the canonical ~1:6 term placental:fetal weight ratio. Coefficients vary by population; central value reproduces Hutcheon's cross-sectional means.

name: Placental ~ fetal weight allometric exponent (b in PW = a * FW^b)
tier_rationale: Tier C from Hutcheon 2012 + Burton 2010: the placental-to-fetal weight relation is mildly sub-linear; exponent ~0.85 reproduces the late-gestation slight decline in placental:fetal ratio.

name: Maternal-fetal IgG transfer ratio, mid-pregnancy baseline
tier_rationale: Tier D (hypothesis-only): FcRn-mediated IgG transfer is qualitatively documented but quantitatively poorly constrained. Palmeira 2012 reviews the rise from low fetal/maternal IgG ratios in T1-T2 to ratios near or above 1 at term; exact kinetics depend on FcRn expression and IgG subclass. DO NOT USE FOR PREDICTION.
notes: Hypothesis-only Tier D parameter. Used in hypothesis-only submodel maternal_fetal_igg_transfer.

name: Maternal-fetal IgG transfer ratio at term
tier_rationale: Tier D (hypothesis-only): Term fetal/maternal IgG ratios typically exceed 1.0 (active FcRn pumping), with wide cohort variation. Palmeira 2012 review; precise kinetics are unsettled. DO NOT USE FOR PREDICTION.

name: Placental 11beta-HSD2 cortisol inactivation fraction
tier_rationale: Tier D (hypothesis-only): Placental 11beta-HSD2 inactivates ~80-90% of cortisol crossing into fetal circulation; the exact saturation kinetics are textbook-qualitative (Benediktsson 1997). Quantitative inactivation fraction varies across individuals and gestational ages and is not well-constrained. DO NOT USE FOR PREDICTION.
notes: Hypothesis-only Tier D parameter used in placental_cortisol_gradient submodel.

name: Intervillous space volume at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Mayhew 2014 stereological morphomics: total intervillous (maternal-blood) space in the term placenta is approximately 130-250 mL, with the bulk of intervillous flow turning over several times per minute given uterine artery flow ~500-700 mL/min. Sterological estimates vary across studies and fixation methods.
notes: Maternal-blood compartment that bathes the chorionic villi; the residence-time view is more useful than absolute volume.

name: Total villous capillary length at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Mayhew 2014 stereological morphomics: the total length of fetal villous capillaries in the term placenta is approximately 280-320 km — one of the more striking quantitative statistics in placental anatomy, illustrating the enormous gas-exchange surface achieved by branching geometry. The Tier-C range reflects stereology technique variation.
notes: If laid end-to-end, term villous capillaries would span the distance from London to Edinburgh.

name: Placental thickness at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Hoddick et al. 1985 J Ultrasound Med: ultrasound series characterising normal placental thickness across gestation. Term thickness centred ~2.5 cm with a normal upper bound of ~4 cm; thicker placentas raise differential including diabetes, hydrops, and infection. Widely cited as the obstetric-ultrasound reference range.
notes: Measured at the cord insertion on a transverse ultrasound view of the placenta.

name: Umbilical cord length at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from Naeye 1985 J Pediatr: large clinicopathological series (>30,000 placentas examined at delivery) establishing the population distribution of cord length. Mean ~55 cm, with the conventional 'short' / 'long' cord thresholds (<35 cm, >80 cm) drawn from the same series. Has remained the canonical reference for cord-length norms.
notes: Cord-length extremes correlate with later neurodevelopmental and obstetric outcomes but the central distribution is what nidus exposes.

name: Syncytiotrophoblast thickness at term
tier_rationale: Tier C from Mayhew 2014 stereology of human placental morphometry: the syncytial layer thins from ~10–12 μm at mid-pregnancy to ~3–5 μm at term, optimising diffusion distance for the rising fetal metabolic demand. Inter-individual variability is substantial; stereological estimates depend on sampling method, motivating Tier C.
notes: Thinning of the materno-fetal barrier is a key mechanism by which the placenta supports late-pregnancy gas exchange.

name: Number of spiral arteries supplying the intervillous space
tier_rationale: Tier C from Pijnenborg et al. 2006 Placenta review of human spiral-artery anatomy: ~80–150 spiral arteries per placenta enter the intervillous space at term, after deep trophoblast invasion and remodelling that converts each from a thick-walled high-resistance vessel to a wide-bore low-resistance conduit. The count is anatomically variable; failed remodelling underlies pre-eclampsia (out of scope for nidus).
notes: Spiral-artery count × per-vessel flow gives the rough order-of-magnitude check on the total uterine artery flow (~600 mL/min at term).

name: Spiral artery diameter at term (remodelled)
tier_rationale: Tier C from Pijnenborg et al. 2006 Placenta review: after physiological trophoblast-driven remodelling, term spiral arteries dilate to ~2–4 mm (from ~0.5 mm non-pregnant), losing their muscular wall and becoming maternal-pressure-passive conduits. The remodelling is incomplete in pre-eclampsia. Reported diameters span a wide range; Tier C reflects the anatomical heterogeneity, not data scarcity.
notes: The dilation factor (~5×) translates by Poiseuille to a >500× drop in single-vessel resistance — the mechanism behind the pregnancy-induced uterine vasodilation.
